creator: mkamm@gmx.net
text:
The ports system unnecessarily refuses to install ports if called by
  non-superusers. The problem is that if the port uses the supplied
  INSTALL_* macros chown and chgrp will be requested and will fail.

  But installing ports by mere mortals makes much sense together with
  a changed prefix (like PREFIX=$HOME PKG_DBDIR=$HOME/pkg) and should
  be possible.

  There is already code for non-superuser operation inside bsd.port.mk
  (like not doing chown -R after extract, not running mtree and
  warning about permissions) but this patch is still missing.
